title: Did Gendo Openly Betray Seele in 30
display_title: Did Gendo Openly Betray Seele in 30
wikitext:
[]: Did Gendo Openly Betray Seele in 3.0?             Quote Post by Damaijin » Wed Dec 03, 2014 9:07 pm When I watched the scene in which Gendo "ritualistically shuts down the Seele monoliths in preparation of FI" (which is how it's described on the movie's page), I had a completely different take on what was happening: The way I saw it, Gendo was killing them off, essentially giving them the finger and saying "the show is mine, now." I saw it as him saying, "you tried to escape death by changing the forms of your souls into these monoliths, but as children who have tasted the fruit of knowledge, you can't escape death, which I deliver to you now. I'll continue your original plan, which you have abandoned like cowards in your pursuit of immortality, and strike down God. Then I'll do what I want from there. (Gendo's too reserved to mwah-ha-ha, otherwise that would have appeared here)" That's also why I thought he considered it a win that he took out "Seele's boy". Because now there's no one else to stop him from amending the Dead Sea Scrolls, which he also told the Monoliths he was going to do even though he told Fuyutsuki earlier that the Monoliths were content because everything was going according to the DSS. Thoughts on this, please? I'd like someone else's take on this if they're willing. I'd like to add as an afterthought that the Seele Monolith's final words reeked to me of "You're still just a puppet, Ikari, and we'll be pulling your strings from beyond the grave."

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Dec 03, 2014 9:21 pm Of course he did, he's the only major character in Q that isn't mentally handicapped. He lied to their faces claiming he'll give them what they always wanted, and SEELE being the bumbling idiots Q made them, gladly let him kill them off before they saw their goal accomplished, basically trusting Gendo's word of all things. It would almost be hilarious if it wasn't so silly.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Dec 03, 2014 10:16 pm Gendo is like Ocelot: the guy is categorically incapable of not screwing someone over every five minutes, and every time he does so his entire game plan manages to seem more and more convoluted and make less and less sense to the point where it just comes across as "ride the wave, wing it, pretend I'm awesome." So in short we should assume he's betraying someone whenever he does anything significant ever.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 04, 2014 12:27 am ^ That's...honestly a pretty accurate assessment, especially New Theatrical Edition Gendo. As far as Gendo mockingly giving Seele what they want; Seele's parting words being the upper hand--that very well could be what's going on. I more or less read the scene as a mix of both (Gendo and Seele are giving each other what they want, but still screwing each other over behind the scenes). I guess FINAL will tell us what the deal is, hopefully. Does make me wonder if those Preview "MP Evas" (assuming they'll be in the final product) are actually from Seele, not Neo-Nerv? I mean, I guess Kaworu "meeting again" with Shinji could be a new clone, BUT, I naturally assumed Kaworu meeting Shinji would be some metaphysical and/or Instrumentality reasons--and Kaworu has his own agenda whether or not he was "Seele's Boy" until the end of Eva: 3.0. Sigh, I really get tired of writing "We'll just we have to wait and see" in all these Rebuild threads.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Dec 04, 2014 2:34 am My opinion? Yeah, Gendo's betraying Seele. Pretend to give the old men what they want, then shut them off-- but then again, perhaps Gendo's been playing the good boy for so long that some of Seele's plans are now his.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Dec 04, 2014 6:12 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Does make me wonder if those Preview "MP Evas" (assuming they'll be in the final product) are actually from Seele, not Neo-Nerv?

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Thu Dec 04, 2014 8:04 am chee wrote: Gendo is like Ocelot: the guy is categorically incapable of not screwing someone over every five minutes

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Dec 04, 2014 7:17 pm ElMariachi wrote: The Eva transport trains in the assembly line have neo-NERV's logo, so it's more probable that the MP-Evas will be under Gendo's control.

[]:             Quote Post by Jimbob » Thu Dec 04, 2014 11:01 pm Well he actually didn't OPENLY betray them. He let them believe he was going to carry their will as he shut them down, instead of mwahahaing at them that the show was his and he just screwed them over. He's clearly gonna betray them, but opted not to rub in their faces for the sake of it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Dec 05, 2014 9:57 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Likely, unless they have Seele code built into them. Remember, when Rei was piloting the Mk.09, Seele took it over at one point.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Fri Dec 05, 2014 7:53 pm Gendou played a hard hand of Let's Pull a Fast One on Joey(FOJ), so you can think of him as an expert con man in that sense. This doesn't come that as that much of a surprise the audience but it still is surprising because he fooled us in an confusing over-the-top way too. FOJ is a political game played with 3 people: the aggressor(AG), the accomplice(AC), and the victim(V). AG(Gendou) tells AC(Seele or Kaworu) that they should pull a fast one on V(Joey, or Shinji in this case). AC gets on board with AG, but at the last moment("the two spears here are the same"), the AC is demoted and forced to play by the V's irritating rules(Kaworu gets his head chopped off, and Gendou affirms "we got SEELE's boy" as the payoff). It probably wasn't terrifically difficult to play this game with Shinji in the victim role, seeing as Shinji wasn't psychologically stable and is also not foreign to that role, and that placed him into the hands of Kaworu, with Gendou manning the whole operation. It might have been challenging to get 13 to go into berserk mode but Shinji was easy enough to manipulate. So basically, yes, Gendou betrayed SEELE. He played SEELE, Kaworu, and Shinji through a string of events efficiently, and turned his back on SEELE in the last few moments. It's a simple game that was, as many games are, played in secret. Games aren't all bad - some games are healthy and often, games are necessary in healthy living due to the restrictions of intimacy. But Gendou wasn't playing a game at that level, and he played a very hard hand for a very big reward. He was able to cover the risks associated with the event too. Think big time cons and scandals, where the thieves make off with millions of dollars. That's what Gendou pulled. Rei was also pulled into this game, but she noticed it happening to her when she remarked "why isn't the link being restored?" One way to make someone consciously irritated is to exert control over them in a way where they recognize they are limited in some way or another. When Rei was not given the autonomy of her machine, she started getting worried. With Asuka's inspiring words - "what do you want to do?" - her ego was restored and she fled. She rose above the game after the feeling of being confined that it produced.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Dec 05, 2014 9:18 pm ElMariachi wrote: I really doubt that Gendo didn't planned anything about the MP-Evas if they are SEELE controlled, so if they appear in Shin Eva, that means that either neo-NERV were the one that builds them or that Gendo took the control of them if it was SEELE who built them. Which in the end is the same, since by killing SEELE and Kaworu, Gendo now have access to all their assets.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 06, 2014 8:34 am ^ Frankly, I don't really see what bringing back SEELE as a menace could bring to Shin Eva: SEELE was defeated in Q, Gendo took the lead as Big Bad, having a remnant of SEELE screwing around will change absolutely nothing for the good guys, since their objective will still be destroying neo-NERV's fortress and all their Evas to prevent any new Impacts.

[]:             Quote Post by Damaijin » Sun Dec 07, 2014 1:00 pm 1. But was Gendo really giving them what they wanted? Did they actually want to "die", or be shut down or whatever? I'd imagine the reason they had themselves transformed was because they wanted to partake of whatever Impact would make them immortal... 2. Is Gendo really the "Big Bad" now? Fuyutsuki's been hinting Gendo's plan has something to do with repairing some of the damage while also fulfilling his own desire to get Yui back. Like he's trying to salvage as much as he can from what's left, and by doing whatever he has to to do it.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Dec 07, 2014 5:13 pm chee wrote: "ride the wave, wing it, pretend I'm awesome."

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Dec 07, 2014 5:13 pm Damaijin wrote: 1. But was Gendo really giving them what they wanted? Did they actually want to "die", or be shut down or whatever? I'd imagine the reason they had themselves transformed was because they wanted to partake of whatever Impact would make them immortal...

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Dec 07, 2014 5:23 pm ElMariachi wrote: Yes, SEELE wanted to die at that moment of the HIP so their souls would be "reaped" alongside the rest of mankind so they would be reborn into a new race of being possessing the Fruit of Life, according to Kaworu's (sparse) explanation of what their version of Instrumentality is in NTE.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Dec 07, 2014 5:50 pm Chuckman wrote: If this is their actual goal and it's possible to achieve it without going horribly wrong, fuck everybody I'm on board.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Dec 07, 2014 11:05 pm ElMariachi wrote: Also, there's also the fact that SEELE didn't asked anyone else about their opinion on the matter and the fact that they arbitrarily decided to sacrifice half of the human population that won't benefit from the HIP so the Angels could be awakened and their plans set in motion.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Dec 08, 2014 12:14 am They probably could have just given everybody acid and skipped the multibillionular genocide and expensive giant robots.
